CP00-819 is a clean descriptor-only RP26 closeout-handoff pack. I independently verified the 86-unit deliverable distribution against the baseline, all row-constant lengths/titles, the descriptor/Hermes/Claude packets (no-write, read-only, non-final), the validator and contract enforcement (`validation.valid:true`, CP00-819 promoted), the test assertions, and the live-plan cursor. The two P3s match the CP00-818 pattern: (1) downstream closeout artifacts not yet generated and validation commands not run under read-only constraints, and (2) a cosmetic dual-listing of `new-session-handoff.md` in changed-file-scope. Reviewed read-only via Read/Grep/Glob.

Production-ready claim is correctly scoped and honest. The manifest declares production_ready:false, runtime_ready:false, status:in_progress, with production_ready_flag="enterprise_saas_review_closeout_handoff_descriptor_verified" and implementation_summary asserting descriptor_only:true, runtime_execution:false, no_real_data:true, writes_product_state:false, local_validation_claims_enterprise_trust:false, human_final_approval_required_for_runtime_opening:true, production_ready_descriptor_verified:false. This is the expected pre-closeout state: the immediately prior closed pack CP00-818 (whose adjudication names CP00-819/RP26.P09.M06.S07 as the next boundary) shows the same shape and flipped production_ready:true only at closeout. The pack makes no runtime or enterprise-trust claim.

Implementation is consistent and well-bounded, verified independently. registry.js ENTERPRISE_SAAS_CP819_PACK_BINDING (86 units, range RP26.P09.M06.S07-RP26.P09.M10.S10, upstream CP00-818, next CP00-820/RP27.P00.M00.S01, flag enterprise_saas_review_closeout_handoff_descriptor_verified) matches the baseline. ENTERPRISE_SAAS_CP819_REQUIREMENTS deliverable_counts {implementation:57, claude_review:12, security_audit:8, test:4, ui:4, hermes_evidence:1}=86 match my own count of the baseline included_units exactly; phase_counts {RP26.P09:86}, micro_phase_row_counts {M06:14,M07:22,M08:20,M09:20,M10:10}, and micro_title_row_counts all match. Row constants verified: REVIEW_CLOSEOUT_SCOPE_ROWS=10, CONTRACT_ROWS=20, HANDOFF_ROWS=22; section bindings (M06=CONTRACT_ROWS.slice(6)=14, M07=HANDOFF_ROWS=22, M08/M09=CONTRACT_ROWS=20, M10=SCOPE_ROWS=10) reproduce the baseline unit titles in order. service.js createEnterpriseSaasCp819ReviewCloseoutHandoffDescriptor builds the descriptor (source EnterpriseSaasCp818ReviewCloseoutApiUiBridgeDescriptor), Hermes packet (receipt_shape "descriptor_only_enterprise_saas_no_write", emits_runtime_receipt:false), Claude packet (read_only:true, promotes_claude_to_final_approval:false), and closeout handoff to CP00-820/RP27.P00.M00.S01; authority_boundary sets claude_is_final_approval:false, local_validation_claims_enterprise_trust:false, human_final_approval_required_for_runtime_opening:true. freezeResult injects the full ENTERPRISE_SAAS_NO_WRITE_ATTESTATION into every output and createCaseSet emits descriptor-only rows with no tenant/SSO/SCIM/secret payloads. validators.js validateCoverage/validateDescriptor enforce distribution/row drift plus a full battery of no-leak guards (no SSO/MFA/SCIM runtime, no cross-tenant routes, no key/secret material, no permission-decision or audit writes, no API/UI execution, no real tenant data, no enterprise-trust claim) and SSO/SCIM sub-descriptor validation; scripts/validate-rp26-enterprise-saas-hardening-contract.mjs chains coverage+descriptor+contract+capabilities+gates+research. The contract promotes current/latest CP00-819 with latest_projection.descriptor=EnterpriseSaasCp819ReviewCloseoutHandoffDescriptor, validation.valid:true, CP00-818 moved to historical. model.test.js asserts coverage.errors==[], by_deliverable deepEqual, 86 rows, no real tenant/permission/audit/API/UI leakage, Hermes no-receipt, Claude read-only/non-final, handoff routing, and the full CP788-CP819 projection chain. index.js re-exports all CP819 symbols; mandatory artifacts sso.js/scim.js present; README documents CP00-819 accurately.

Authority and CP178+ review-hardening boundaries hold. Claude stays read_only_independent_reviewer (allowed_claude_tools=[Read,Grep,Glob]; not final approver); enterprise-trust closure is external to this receipt and the pack does not claim it; Hermes is descriptor-only control-plane handling, not embedded in product runtime. forbidden_review_evidence is defined (not_logged_in, malformed_json, usage_limit_error, permission_denial, write_tool_used, runtime_receipt_claim); readiness auth-preflight passed (exit 0, 1444-byte stdout, valid JSON {"ok":true}, no stderr); review-request mandates read_only + JSON-schema output; validation-report confirms summary_only_review_allowed:false (Risk C + security/audit + review-evidence), forbidden_tools_present:false, active_untracked_implementation_files_omitted:[]. The live plan cursor (live_state pack CP00-818 closed, next_subphase RP26.P09.M06.S07) confirms CP00-819 is the correct next pack, and changed-file-scope isolates the 7 active enterprise implementation refs + docs/closeout-packs/cp00-819 from unrelated dirty files (UI control-room, workbook, docx, LDIP invalid-attempt).

Two P3 informational notes only; no P0/P1/P2. This is the expected pre-closeout state: this review is the gate that precedes generating command-evidence (validation command results), claude-review-result, construction-inspection, and adjudication, and flips production_ready/runtime status, exactly as the prior closed pack CP00-818 demonstrates (it now has all five docs artifacts plus raw-output/review-receipt). As a read-only reviewer I could not run npm run closeout-pack:validate / npm test / npm run validate / npm run weighted:validate / git diff --check etc., so command/test PASS is established by source consistency (registry/service/validators/contract/tests all internally aligned and assert errors==[]) and prior-pack precedent rather than fresh execution.","recommendation":"Proceed. The authoritative pack scope is unaffected: active_pack_implementation_refs lists only the 7 enterprise files (contracts/enterprise-saas-hardening-contract.json, packages/enterprise/{README.md,src/registry.js,src/service.js,src/validators.js,test/model.test.js}, scripts/validate-rp26-enterprise-saas-hardening-contract.mjs) and active_pack_required_untracked_files is docs/closeout-packs/cp00-819; the manifest worktree_transition_policy also treats new-session-handoff.md as unrelated, and validation-report confirms active_untracked_implementation_files_omitted=[] and forbidden_tools_present=false. Same cosmetic labeling carried over from CP00-818 (R02). Minor related nit: changed-file-scope.active_pack_required_untracked_files lists only docs/closeout-packs/cp00-819 while the manifest worktree_transition_policy also lists artifacts/closeout-pack-claude-review/cp00-819 — both directories exist and are populated, so no scope impact.","recommendation":"Optionally tighten changed-file-scope classification so a file is not listed in both untracked_implementation_files and unrelated_dirty_files_preserved, and align the active_pack_required_untracked_files lists with the manifest; no scope or boundary impact.","post_review_disposition":"cosmetic_labeling_inconsistency_not_blocking"}],"finding_counts":{"p0":0,"p1":0,"p2":0,"p3":2,"reported_p0":0,"reported_p1":0,"reported_p2":0,"reported_p3":2}},"terminal_reason":"completed","fast_mode_state":"off","uuid":"48d85a4f-bf74-419b-8f83-62f68454f315"}
